entries.title: Project origin — first commits
entries.body: Day zero. The first lines of code that would become Haven were written. A mobile map for No Man's Sky on iOS. None of what exists today existed yet — not the Extractor, not the platform, not the rack. Everything starts here.

entries.title: Data architecture rebuilt in a day
entries.body: Six phases of data structure work shipped in a single sitting. The Control Room database, the wizard database, full integration, stress testing under 100,000 records. The day the architecture finally clicked into place.

entries.title: Control Room beta ships
entries.body: The first version of the Haven Control Room admin interface is functional. Discord bot integration wired in. The submission workflow that exists today starts in this week.

entries.title: Discord goes live with the platform
entries.body: The Voyager's Haven Discord community officially connects to the new platform. Members can now see their discoveries on the map and submit through the wizard. Real members, real data, real feedback — the project becomes a community project.

entries.title: UI theming and Pi prep
entries.body: Real visual polish lands — consistent buttons, cards, modals. Same week, the codebase starts being prepared for life on a Raspberry Pi instead of a laptop. The platform begins growing up.

entries.title: Master-Haven monorepo
entries.body: The original Haven codebase gets reorganized into a single home: Master-Haven. Every project — the platform, the Extractor, the Keeper — lives under one roof from this point forward.

entries.title: Cross-component update push
entries.body: Holiday week brought a wave of updates across the Extractor, the Memory Browser, the Save Watcher, and Haven's own API. The pieces start lining up.

entries.title: Major project update
entries.body: A significant feature push to start the year — documentation refreshed, README brought current with what the platform actually does. The first month of building toward havenmap.online.

entries.title: Big features and cleanup
entries.body: A series of feature additions and code cleanups. Planet Atlas integration updated, mobile views refined, the platform feels noticeably more polished by month's end.

entries.title: Pi 8GB migration begins
entries.body: Haven moves from the smaller 2GB Pi to the 8GB. Docker installed. The tri-synergy architecture — Haven Control Room, Extractor, Keeper — finally has a server that can hold all of it.

entries.title: Haven moves to its permanent home
entries.body: Migration from temporary tunnels to a dedicated Pi server at havenmap.online. Faster, more reliable, and ours — not rented. The platform has lived here ever since.

entries.title: First rack assembled
entries.body: A 3D-printed 8U rack in PETG, switch mounted, Pis nested in their slots. The Haven homelab gets a real physical home for the first time — not just code in containers, but hardware you can put your hands on.

entries.title: Exchange concept born
entries.body: The first version of the Exchange gets sketched out: a community currency, member-run businesses, a simulated blockchain to keep transactions honest.

entries.title: Travelers Collective designed
entries.body: A central exploration database for Haven, with the architecture to grow. Temporal versioning, contribution tracking, room for partner communities someday.

entries.title: Per-user API keys, end-to-end Extractor integration
entries.body: Every Extractor user now has their own API key, generated on first export. Connection between the Extractor and the platform is fully wired — submit a discovery in-game, see it in Haven minutes later. The pipeline that defines the project now works end-to-end.

entries.title: Auto-updater built into the Extractor
entries.body: The Extractor now updates itself on launch — no more manually grabbing new versions. Stale galaxy data replaced. Star colors and spectral classes finally captured properly.

entries.title: Community pages added
entries.body: Each partner community in Haven now has its own page — activity timelines, member counts, contributions over time. The platform starts feeling less like a database and more like a place where communities live.

entries.title: Photo compression on upload
entries.body: Every photo uploaded to Haven now gets compressed automatically. Faster page loads, less storage, same visual quality.

entries.title: Major codebase cleanup
entries.body: A multi-pass refactor across the platform. Hundreds of lines of unused code removed, dead endpoints retired, performance improved on slower devices. The kind of work no one sees, but everyone benefits from.

entries.title: Travelers Exchange goes live in Docker
entries.body: The Exchange container deployed to the Pi alongside Haven Control Room. First time the platform has more than one application running side by side. The shape of the homelab starts to take form.

entries.title: Domain settled, banking and GDP designed
entries.body: The Exchange found its proper home at travelers-exchange.online. Same day, the banking system and dynamic GDP-based pricing got designed — member-run banks, real interest mechanics, and shop prices that respond to a nation's economic activity.

entries.title: Viobot moves into the Haven homelab
entries.body: The Viobot moderation bot — built and maintained by art3mis and Goat from IEA — gets its own home in our Docker stack on the Pi. Same bot, same maintainers, just hosted by us now.

entries.title: War Room and refinements across the platform
entries.body: Updated the War Room with new tooling for ongoing campaigns, plus quieter improvements throughout: cleaner mobile layouts, faster system search, and a redesigned submission flow for fauna entries.

entries.title: Conversations continuing with Had.Sh
entries.body: Ongoing discussions with the leadership of Had.Sh about possible future data sharing. Nothing formal yet — just two communities figuring out how we might work together.

entries.title: Reframing the Exchange around contribution
entries.body: After conversations with members and partner community leaders, we're reshaping the Exchange so that earning currency is tied directly to contribution — cataloging systems, building bases, documenting fauna. The structure is being rewritten now.

entries.tag: Travelers Exchange · The Keeper · Haven UI
entries.title: Big Update and closing the circle
entries.body: Travelers Exchange V3 design is now live and has onboarding docs for new users, how it actually works, and Nation Leader onboarding - Keeper is now fully functional and being polished for better user experience + two new slash commands - Haven UI now has unique galaxy poster images that are weekly updated to show civs on the map + personal stats card inside the profile tabs

entries.title: Haven Docs goes live
entries.body: A real documentation hub at havenmap.online/haven-ui/docs. Three guides on day one — Getting Started for new members, For Civilization Leaders for community leads thinking about partnering, and Under the Hood, a long-form technical doc that includes WhrStrsG's community guide to the glyph and portal coordinate system. Each long-form doc has a numbered sidebar with scroll-tracking. The Docs button on the landing page now points here. The Changelog page hasn't moved.
